title: 10 best herbal creams for hyperacidity

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice creams helps with hyperacidity because they contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that has been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties. When used in conjunction with other natural ingredients, it can help soothe and calm the stomach lining, reducing inflammation and alleviating symptoms of hyperacidity such as heartburn and acid reflux. The creams also work to neutralize excess stomach acid, providing quick relief from discomfort and indigestion.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: This compound helps to reduce inflammation and soothe stomach ulcers caused by acid reflux, thereby providing relief from heartburn and other digestive issues.
Flavonoids: These antioxidants neutralize free radicals and protect the mucous membranes in the stomach, reducing irritation and discomfort associated with hyperacidity.
Phenolic acids: These compounds have antimicrobial properties that help to inhibit the growth of harmful bacteria in the gut, which can contribute to acid reflux and other digestive problems.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps to reduce inflammation and soothe digestive issues associated with hyperacidity.
Rhyzomes: Containing triterpenoid saponins, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help alleviate symptoms of hyperacidity.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh or dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots in the morning after sunrise weighing approximately one kilogram.
Dry the harvested roots in shade or by air circulation at room temperature for seven to ten days.
Grind 200 grams of the dried roots into fine powder using mortar and pestle.
Combine the powdered glycyrrhiza glabra with 500 milliliters of sweet almond oil and 20 milliliters of beeswax in a heat-resistant container.
Heat the mixture at low temperature for one hour to melt the beeswax then cool and whip until creamy consistency is achieved.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe creams helps with hyperacidity because they possess soothing and anti-inflammatory properties that calm and protect the digestive tract. The gel extracted from aloe vera plants has natural buffering agents that help neutralize excess acid in the stomach, providing quick relief from heartburn and indigestion symptoms. Additionally, aloe's antimicrobial properties can help eliminate harmful bacteria that contribute to hyperacidity, promoting a healthy balance of gut flora and reducing inflammation in the digestive tract.
remedy_properties: Soothing and anti-inflammatory effects: Aloe vera's ability to calm and reduce inflammation in the digestive tract helps to alleviate symptoms of hyperacidity, such as heartburn and acid reflux.
Antacid properties: Aloe vera contains a number of compounds that have antacid properties, which can help neutralize excess stomach acid and relieve symptoms of hyperacidity.
Probiotic effects: Herbal aloe creams often contain probiotics, which can help restore the balance of gut bacteria and improve digestion, reducing the production of excess stomach acid.
remedy_parts: Roots: Aloe roots are used to create a soothing gel that can help neutralize stomach acid and reduce inflammation in the digestive tract.
Leaves: The thick, fleshy leaves of the aloe plant contain a high concentration of mucilages, which provide a protective barrier against stomach acid and irritants.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh aloe vera gel from mature leaves.
Mix 200ml of aloe vera gel with 50g of dried and powdered licorice root extract in a stainless steel bowl.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes while stirring occasionally until the powder is fully dissolved.
Allow the mixture to cool and then add 100ml of coconut oil and 50ml of olive oil.
Whip the mixture until it reaches a smooth and creamy consistency using an electric mixer.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger creams helps with hyperacidity because they contain natural anti-inflammatory compounds like gingerols and shogaols, which have been shown to reduce inflammation in the stomach lining. These compounds also help to neutralize excess acid production by stimulating the release of digestive enzymes that break down food more efficiently. Additionally, herbal ginger creams can soothe and calm the digestive tract, reducing symptoms such as heartburn and indigestion associated with hyperacidity.

Digestive enzymes: Ginger contains digestive enzymes that aid in protein digestion and reduce gastric acid production, which can help neutralize excess acidity in the stomach.
Antioxidant properties: Ginger's antioxidant properties help protect the stomach lining from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to hyperacidity and inflammation.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes are the underground stems that store nutrients, which contain compounds like gingerol and shogaol that have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in soothing digestive issues related to hyperacidity.
Barks: The barks of the ginger plant contain bioactive compounds like flavonoids and terpenes, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of hyperacidity.
remedy_recipe: Measure 20 grams of dried ginger root into a clean glass bowl.
Add 100 milliliters of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil slowly stirring until well combined.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler for 30 minutes at a temperature range of 60 to 70 degrees celsius.
Allow the mixture to cool down completely then strain it through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass jar.
Add 10 grams of beeswax and 20 milliliters of vitamin e oil slowly stirring until fully incorporated and whipped.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tea creams helps with hyperacidity because they contain a blend of soothing herbs that work synergistically to neutralize stomach acid and calm digestive discomfort. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh camellia sinensis leaves and rinse them gently in cold water to remove dirt and impurities.
Steep one teaspoon of dried camellia sinensis leaves or two teaspoons of fresh leaves in boiling water for ten minutes.
Strain the tea mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass bowl, discarding solids.
Mix four tablespoons of beeswax and two tablespoons of coconut oil with the cooled tea liquid, stirring until fully incorporated and melted.
Pour the cream base into sterilized jars, seal tightly, and allow to cool and set for at least 30 minutes before use.

remedy_recipe: Weigh out one hundred grams of dried taraxacum officinale leaves in a clean glass bowl.
Add two hundred milliliters of cold-pressed olive oil to the bowl and stir until well combined.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler at low heat for thirty-five minutes, then let it cool.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass jar, squeezing out as much liquid as possible.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold creams helps with hyperacidity because of its unique combination of anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried calendula officinalis flowers at the peak of bloom for optimal potency and flavor.
Dry the harvested flowers in a single layer on paper bags or clean cloth sheets for 1-2 weeks.
Combine 20g of dried calendula officinalis flowers with 100ml of boiling water to create an infusion that steeps for 30 minutes.
Mix the cooled infusion with 200g of unrefined coconut oil and 50g of beeswax, stirring until the mixture reaches a smooth consistency after 1-2 hours.
Fill sterilized containers with the finished cream, allowing it to mature for at least 24 hours before use or storage for up to 6 months.

Mix the cooled herbal infusion with two tablespoons of beeswax, three tablespoons of coconut oil, and one teaspoon of vitamin e oil in a double boiler.
Stir the mixture until it reaches a smooth consistency then pour into sterilized containers and refrigerate at 2-8°c for up to six months.

remedy_recipe: Harvest melissa officinalis leaves and flowers for a total of 2 ounces by cutting them at the height of flowering.
Dry the harvested botanicals using a warm air circulation method for 24 hours or until crumbly.
Combine 16 ounces of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil with 4 ounces of dried melissa officinalis in a double boiler.
Steep the mixture for 6 hours and then strain it through cheesecloth to collect the infused oil.
Mix 1/2 cup of beeswax with 1 cup of the infused oil and whip until smooth, cooling the mixture to an ambient temperature before pouring into containers.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 20g of dried ginkgo biloba leaves and grind them into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar pestle for 2 minutes.
Mix the ginkgo biloba powder with 100ml of cold-pressed sweet almond oil and 10ml of beeswax in a double boiler for 30 minutes.
Heat the mixture to a temperature of 60°c over low heat, stirring occasionally, until the beeswax is fully dissolved and the mixture is smooth and consistent.
Remove the mixture from the heat and add 5ml of vitamin e oil and 2 drops of lavender essential oil; stir well to combine and let cool for 1 hour.
Pour the cooled mixture into sterilized glass jars, seal tightly, and store in the refrigerator for up to 6 months or freeze for up to a year.

Allow the mixture to cool and solidify at room temperature before transferring it to an airtight container for storage.

The menthol in peppermint helps to neutralize stomach acid, while the anti-inflammatory properties of chamomile soothe and calm the digestive tract. Licorice root, rich in glycyrrhizin, coats and protects the mucous membranes, reducing inflammation and irritation caused by excess acidity. This natural combination provides fast and effective relief from hyperacidity symptoms, promoting a healthy gut and overall well-being.
remedy_properties: Alkalinity: New Jersey Tea contains alkaline compounds that help neutralize excess stomach acid, providing relief from heartburn and indigestion.
Antioxidant Activity: The antioxidants present in New Jersey Tea have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract and alleviate symptoms of hyperacidity.

Soothing Astringent Properties: New Jersey Tea's astringent compounds help to calm and soothe irritated mucous membranes in the stomach, reducing inflammation and discomfort caused by excess acid production.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, which help soothe and calm the digestive system.
Barks: Contains alkaloids that can help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with hyperacidity.
Seeds: Rich in fatty acids and amino acids, which provide a natural barrier to protect the skin from acidity-related irritation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried leaves of ceanothus americanus and grind them into fine powder using mortar and pestle method within minutes.
Combine ground ceanothus americana with organic coconut oil in ratio of 1:3 and heat the mixture to 150°f within an hour.
Allow the mixture to cool down to room temperature while stirring occasionally then strain it through cheesecloth within two hours.
Mix the filtered oil with beeswax in proportion of 70% oil to 30% wax and melt them together at 180°f for ten minutes.
Pour the melted mixture into sterilized containers and let it solidify at room temperature over several hours before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile creams helps with hyperacidity because of its natural soothing properties, which effectively calm and relax the digestive system. The anti-inflammatory compounds present in chamomile, such as apigenin and luteolin, help to reduce inflammation and irritation in the stomach lining, reducing symptoms of heartburn and acid reflux. Additionally, chamomile's antispasmodic properties ease muscle contractions in the stomach and esophagus, allowing digestive acids to flow more smoothly and efficiently, providing relief from hyperacidity-related discomforts.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as apigenin and luteolin, help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can alleviate symptoms of hyperacidity like heartburn and indigestion.
Sotheing property: The soothing and calming effects of chamomile's flavonoids and terpenoids can help calm irritated stomach tissues and reduce discomfort associated with acid reflux and indigestion.

Antioxidant property: Chamomile's antioxidant properties can neutralize free radicals that may contribute to hyperacidity by reducing oxidative stress in the digestive system, which can lead to inflammation and tissue damage.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in apigenin, a flavonoid that helps to reduce inflammation and soothe digestive issues associated with hyperacidity.
Flowers: Abundant in azulene compounds, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can calm the stomach and reduce acidity.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried chamomile flowers from a reputable source and measure 50 grams into a clean glass jar.
Combine the dried chamomille with 100 milliliters of organic sweet almond oil in a double boiler or a heat-resistant glass bowl.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the chamomile flowers have infused their essence into the oil.
Strain the infusion through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids and preserving the infused oil.
Whip the infused oil with 50 grams of beeswax and 20 milliliters of coconut oil in a separate bowl until smooth and creamy, then pour into sterilized jars and seal tightly.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian creams helps with hyperacidity because they contain a natural ingredient that soothes and calms the digestive system. remedy_properties: Sedative properties: Valerian's sedative effects help to calm the digestive system, reducing inflammation and soothing acidity in the stomach and gut.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 200g of dried valeriana officinalis root and grind it into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 2 minutes.
Combine the valeriana powder with 100g of coconut oil in a double boiler over low heat for 10 minutes.
Add 50g of beeswax pellets to the mixture and stir until fully incorporated for an additional 5 minutes.
Allow the mixture to cool and thicken at room temperature for 30 minutes before whipping it into a creamy consistency with a hand mixer for 2 minutes.
Transfer the valeriana cream to sterilized containers and store them in the refrigerator for up to 6 months or freeze for up to 1 year.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for hyperacidity are topical treatments that contain natural ingredients, such as aloe vera, chamomile, and peppermint, which help to neutralize stomach acid and provide relief from symptoms of hyperacidity. These creams work by soothing the digestive tract, reducing inflammation, and promoting healing. Examples of herbal creams that help with hyperacidity include Aloe Vera Cream and Digestive Balm. By using these creams, individuals can find relief from heartburn, bloating, and discomfort, ultimately improving their quality of life.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat hyperacidity with herbal creams is to opt for a cream containing Aloe vera gel, which helps neutralize stomach acid and soothe the digestive tract. Another effective option is a cream infused with Licorice root extract, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can alleviate heartburn symptoms. Additionally, creams featuring Marshmallow root extract can provide long-lasting relief from acidity by coating the mucous membranes in the digestive tract.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to hyperacidity that are treated with herbal creams are eczema, acne, rosacea, and minor burns. Herbal creams can soothe and calm irritated skin, reduce inflammation, and promote healing. For example, aloe vera is often used to treat minor burns and sunburns, while tea tree oil is commonly used to combat acne and rosacea due to its antimicrobial properties.
